note: Round-2 follow-up experiments (dot=0, 3000 cells, 20x, real keystrokes). Median input in ms. 'starved' = cells never painted in the window; for bounded/pull under storm this is a short-window artifact of a fair cursor mid-sweep, NOT permanent starvation (see applies-per-cell: rq concentrates paints on ~8 cells at up to 13x while the rest get 0; bounded/simple/pull spread fairly at <=1x per sweep). Reproduce: node examples/render-queue-lab/round2.mjs [starve|compare|fidelity].

rq.verdict: STARVES — same ~16 front cells repainted, 2984 never painted

bounded.verdict: fair — cursor sweeps all, <=1x each, rest not-yet-reached

fidelity_paintonly_dashboard.note: rq-vs-off median-input ratio, headless (software raster) vs headed (real compositor via xvfb). Single-case, throttle drifted 24-36x, so noisy; the ~3200ms frame floor persists in BOTH paths.
